behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (request builder -> API client), not model judgment. A pure unit test of a helper could miss dropped fields during wiring. Rejected System because behavior text is about request contents, which can be verified with an in-process fake client (no real OS/network requirement). Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier service to a fake API client that records outbound payloads. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert the sent request includes all labels exactly once in the expected field.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at response handoff (API adapter output -> normalization/validation -> public return). Unit tests of only normalization logic may miss wiring that bypasses validation. Rejected Agentic because this behavior is fully testable with deterministic stubbed API output and does not require model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets deterministic normalization logic for identical inputs; no module interaction or I/O is needed. Rejected Integration because behavior text scopes to the normalization step itself, a single local function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real AI provider outputs across repeated live calls; stubs cannot reveal model-side variability or drift. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for the same document/taxonomy, capture provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when raw provider response meaningfully changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration between tokenizer/budget policy and dispatch path (must prevent/modify outbound call). A unit on token math alone can miss dispatch still happening. Rejected System because fake tokenizer/client can reproduce this without real network/OS.
behaviors.test_description: Configure tight token budget, call classify() with oversized document, and assert behavior per policy (truncated payload sent or request rejected) and that dispatch path matches policy expectations.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider-reported usage from the external AI API; mocks can mask tokenizer/provider behavior differences and always pass. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly depends on provider-reported live usage, not only local preflight checks.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents and assert provider-reported total tokens per call do not exceed configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration (error handling + backoff + re-dispatch) across local modules. Deterministic stubs for 429 and fake clock/timer are sufficient. Rejected System because real network/rate-limit infrastructure is not required to validate wait-and-retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Fake API returns rate-limit error on first call and success on second; run classify() with controlled clock and assert a wait/backoff occurs, retry is attempted, and final result is returned without surfacing rate-limit error.
